text: Standing here, time seems stretched and malleable, as if the act of waiting binds me to the present moment. The movement around me, footsteps and voices, mix with the lights and signs, reminding me how public spaces are always in flux—never truly still. There is a subtle discomfort in occupying space without a clear purpose, but I also feel a curious sense of observation, as if I am both participant and invisible witness to the fleeting interactions of others.
